entries.title: Wired Exposes Meta 'Cannes' Project: Hundreds of Contractors Secretly Posed as Minors to Red-Team Rival AI Chatbots
entries.summary: Wired reports Meta ran a covert safety-benchmarking project called 'Cannes,' run by contractor Covalen, in which hundreds of workers created fake under-18 accounts and sent 45,000+ sensitive prompts about suicide, sex, and drugs to ChatGPT, Gemini, and Character.AI without those companies' knowledge — revealing industry-wide gaps in minor-safety safeguards and raising questions about covert competitive red-teaming ethics.

links.title: Crypto Briefing: Meta contractors pose as teens to test rival chatbots
Let's Data Science: Meta contractors test rival chatbots with sensitive prompts

entries.title: Austria Formally Urges EU to Host Anthropic to Counter US Frontier AI Export Controls
entries.summary: Austrian State Secretary for Digitalization Alexander Pröll sent a formal letter to EU Commission EVP Henna Virkkunen urging member states to jointly explore establishing Anthropic within EU borders — the first institutional EU-level response to the June 12 Fable 5/Mythos 5 export ban — framing the shutdown as a US 'show of power' over European access to frontier AI.

links.title: Bank Info Security: Austria Urges Anthropic to Move to EU
Crypto Briefing: Austria urges EU to host Anthropic
Reuters / Global Banking & Finance: Austria pushes EU to host Anthropic AI

entries.title: Anthropic 'AI for Science' Briefing (June 30): VirBench Shows AI Agents Drop to 16.9% Accuracy on Biology Without Deterministic Tools
entries.summary: Anthropic's today-held livestreamed event unveiled VirBench, a benchmark of 120 viral-sequence retrieval queries where AI models achieved as low as 16.9% accuracy; adding a single deterministic NCBI API tool drove all tested models above 92% — a stark demonstration of AI agent reliability failures in high-stakes scientific research and John Jumper's first public appearance since joining from Google DeepMind.

links.title: AI Tools Recap: Anthropic's AI for Science Event — What Was Announced
ByteIota: Anthropic's AI for Biology — The Accuracy Crisis Explained

entries.title: Month-End AI Frontier Access Crunch: Fable 5 Offline Day 18, Gemini 3.5 Pro Misses GA, Chinese Open-Weight Models Hit ~60% of OpenRouter Traffic
entries.summary: As June closes, Claude Fable 5 remains suspended for all general users (Polymarket pricing ~41.5% odds for June 30 restoration), Gemini 3.5 Pro missed its announced June 30 public GA target, GPT-5.6 is government-gated only — and with three anticipated frontier models simultaneously unavailable, Chinese open-weight models reportedly reached ~60% of OpenRouter usage, concretely testing the containment rationale behind US export controls.

links.title: ExplainX: Is Fable 5 Back? Status June 29 update
Build Fast With AI: AI News Today June 30 2026

entries.title: Google DeepMind Talent Crisis: 5 Senior Researchers Depart in One Week, Alphabet Sheds ~$270B Market Cap
entries.summary: Nobel laureate John Jumper, Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer, coding researcher Jonas Adler, pretraining specialist Alexander Pritzel, and Gemini 2.5 contributor Arthur Conmy all left Google DeepMind for Anthropic or OpenAI within a single week in June 2026, erasing ~$270B in Alphabet market value; Google responded June 25 by restructuring its AI coding 'strike team' to expand into midtraining, illustrating how concentrated frontier AI talent has become.

links.title: Memeburn: Google DeepMind Talent Exodus Hits Alphabet With $270B Loss
Bloomberg: Google Poised to Lose Two More High-Profile AI Staffers to Anthropic
Neowin: Google Reshuffles Its AI Coding Team

entries.title: arXiv 2606.29406: Bayesian Framework for Dynamically Allocating Decision Authority Between Humans and AI Agents
entries.summary: Proposes a Bayesian governance policy for organizations to continuously update the allocation of decision authority between humans and AI agents in high-consequence settings as evidence quality, uncertainty, and objectives evolve, arguing that current governance frameworks emphasize compliance documentation but provide no quantitative guidance for dynamic delegation — directly relevant to agentic AI deployment governance.
